title: OptiDesign: Advanced Experimental Design for Breeding Trials
text: Experimental design fundamental component plant breeding agronomic research. ability accurately estimate genetic effects, compare treatments, predict performance depends critically field trials constructed. Traditional designs randomized complete block designs (RCBD) alpha-lattice designs often assume: regular block structures balanced replication independence spatial genetic effects However, modern breeding programs face increasingly complex constraints: large numbers genotypes limited field capacity heterogeneous environments availability genomic pedigree information OptiDesign addresses challenges integrating: flexible field layout construction genetic structure (family, pedigree, genomic) spatial-aware arrangement optional mixed-model efficiency evaluation unified design framework.

title: OptiDesign: Advanced Experimental Design for Breeding Trials
text: Let: yy vector observed phenotypes gg genetic effects ee residual errors consider mixed model: y=Xβ+Zg+e y = X\beta + Zg + e : XX design matrix fixed effects ZZ incidence matrix genotypes g∼N(0,σg2K)g \sim N(0, \sigma_g^2 K) e∼N(0,R)e \sim N(0, R) design problem consists choosing XX ZZ field layout construction optimize: precision β̂\hat{\beta} prediction accuracy ĝ\hat{g}

title: OptiDesign: Advanced Experimental Design for Breeding Trials
text: Field plots spatially structured. Residuals rarely independent : row effects column effects spatial autocorrelation soil gradients management effects Supported residual structures include: “IID” “AR1” “AR1xAR1”

title: OptiDesign: Advanced Experimental Design for Breeding Trials
text: Entries can grouped based : family labels pedigree relationship matrix (AA) genomic relationship matrix (GG GRM) allows design : reduce clustering related genotypes improve diversity spread reduce confounding spatial genetic signals

title: OptiDesign: Advanced Experimental Design for Breeding Trials
text: prep_famoptg() constructs block-based design checks repeated every block, non-check treatments allocated according specified replication levels. Depending input structure, function can generate: augmented design partially replicated (p-rep) design RCBD-type design Let: CC = number checks BB = number blocks rir_i = replication number non-check treatment ii basic structure : checks replicated blocks non-check treatments distributed across blocks according replication levels non-check treatment appears per block last rule important. example, treatment replication 2 number blocks least 2, two replicates placed two different blocks rather repeated within block.

title: OptiDesign: Advanced Experimental Design for Breeding Trials
text: function can represent different design classes depending inputs: Augmented design: p_rep_treatments NULL empty test entries unreplicated checks appear blocks. Partially replicated design: non-check entries replicated others unreplicated. RCBD-type design: non-check treatments replication number greater 1 spread across blocks. addition, n_blocks equal replication number non-check treatments.

title: OptiDesign
text: OptiDesign provides advanced tools constructing optimized experimental field designs plant breeding agronomic experiments. package integrates: field layout construction genetic structure (family, pedigree, genomic relationships) optional spatial dispersion optimization optional mixed-model efficiency evaluation single, flexible workflow.

title: OptiDesign
text: many breeding programs, experimental design treated purely logistical step. However, design choices strongly affect: precision estimates prediction accuracy ability separate genetic effects robustness field heterogeneity OptiDesign allows users explicitly control aspects design stage, rather correcting later analysis.

title: OptiDesign
text: OptiDesign built around three key ideas:

title: OptiDesign
text: Designs reflect trials actually implemented: fixed grid layouts field-book ordering serpentine movement contiguous replicate structure

title: OptiDesign
text: Entries can arranged based : family labels pedigree relationships (matrix) genomic relationships (GRM) allows: reduction local relatedness improved sampling genetic diversity better estimation genetic effects

title: OptiDesign
text: Designs can evaluated directly using mixed-model principles: fixed-effect precision (BLUEs) random-effect prediction (BLUP / GBLUP / PBLUP) spatial residual structures (IID, AR1, AR1×AR1)

title: OptiDesign
text: Construct repeated-check block designs flexible replication, including: augmented designs partially replicated (p-rep) designs RCBD-type repeated-check designs : checks included every block entries may replicated others may unreplicated

title: OptiDesign
text: Use function : many entries limited resources want checks every block want balance replication coverage working early- intermediate-stage breeding trials want flexible design framework covering augmented, p-rep, balanced layouts

title: OptiDesign
text: Flexible replication per entry Block-wise allocation treatments (treatment repeats within block) augmented designs p-rep designs balanced repeated-check (RCBD-type) designs family labels genomic relationship matrix (GRM) pedigree matrix () Optional dispersion optimization reduce clustering related entries Optional efficiency diagnostics mixed models

what: Create a fixed-grid alpha row-column design with unequal incomplete blocks and checks in every block — alpha_rc_stream
title: Create a fixed-grid alpha row-column design with unequal incomplete blocks and checks in every block — alpha_rc_stream
text: alpha_rc_stream() intended practical field situations : overall field size fixed advance, replicate boundaries determined field-book order rather rigid rectangular subfields, replicate 2 begins exactly replicate 1 ends traversal stream, incomplete blocks may differ size, checks must repeated every incomplete block, entries must appear exactly replicate, leftover field cells remain unused appear end stream. function: Builds full field stream using order serpentine. Determines many incomplete blocks can supported replicate. Divides replicate incomplete blocks. Allocates checks entries subject block plan. Arranges entries heuristically reduce clustering similar materials. Leaves surplus cells trailing NA. Optionally performs dispersion optimization. Optionally computes mixed-model efficiency diagnostics.

title: Create a fixed-grid alpha row-column design with unequal incomplete blocks and checks in every block — alpha_rc_stream
code: alpha_rc_stream(   check_treatments,   check_families,   entry_treatments,   entry_families,   n_reps,   n_rows,   n_cols,   order = "column",   serpentine = FALSE,   seed = NULL,   attempts = 5000,   warn_and_correct = TRUE,   fix_rows = TRUE,   cluster_source = c("Family", "GRM", "A"),   GRM = NULL,   A = NULL,   id_map = NULL,   cluster_method = c("kmeans", "hclust"),   cluster_seed = 1,   cluster_attempts = 25,   n_pcs_use = Inf,   min_entry_slots_per_block = 8,   max_blocks_per_rep = NULL,   eval_efficiency = FALSE,   treatment_effect = c("random", "fixed"),   prediction_type = c("none", "IID", "GBLUP", "PBLUP"),   K = NULL,   line_id_map = NULL,   varcomp = list(sigma_e2 = 1, sigma_g2 = 1, sigma_rep2 = 1, sigma_ib2 = 1, sigma_r2 = 1,     sigma_c2 = 1),   check_as_fixed = TRUE,   residual_structure = c("IID", "AR1", "AR1xAR1"),   rho_row = 0,   rho_col = 0,   spatial_engine = c("auto", "sparse", "dense"),   dense_max_n = 5000,   eff_trace_samples = 80,   eff_full_max = 400,   check_placement = c("systematic", "random"),   check_position_pattern = c("spread", "corners_first"),   use_dispersion = FALSE,   dispersion_source = c("K", "A", "GRM"),   dispersion_radius = 1,   dispersion_iters = 2000,   dispersion_seed = 1,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Create a fixed-grid alpha row-column design with unequal incomplete blocks and checks in every block — alpha_rc_stream
text: check_treatments Character vector check IDs. Every check placed exactly every incomplete block. argument always active central design repeated checks define benchmark structure across blocks replicates. Use trial requires standard controls benchmark entries every incomplete block. Example use case: trial 3 commercial checks must appear incomplete blocks. check_families Character vector length check_treatments. Family labels checks. used directly cluster_source = "Family" also stored output field book settings. argument must align exactly check_treatments. Use checks belong known families control groups, user wants family labels preserved output. entry_treatments Character vector distinct entries. entry appears exactly per replicate. main set test entries evaluated. Use breeding lines, hybrids, progeny, candidate materials represented replicate. Entries must overlap check_treatments. entry_families Character vector length entry_treatments. Family labels entries. used directly cluster_source = "Family". cluster_source %% c("GRM", ""), still help determine target number clusters among entries. Use family structure matters spacing, grouping, interpretation. n_reps Integer giving number replicates. replicate defined contiguous segment global stream. Use replicates stronger replication needed entry precision, provided field can still support repeated checks every block. argument affects: total number used plots, length replicate segment, total number times entries appear. n_rows Integer giving number field rows. field size fixed function never altered. Use reflect true row dimension physical field. directly affects: total field capacity, global traversal stream, spatial coordinates returned layout_matrix field_book. n_cols Integer giving number field columns. field size fixed function never altered. Use reflect true column dimension physical field. Together n_rows, determines field capacity stream structure. order Character specifying global traversal order. Allowed values: "column": column-major traversal, "row": row-major traversal. determines: order global field stream, replicate boundaries occur, incomplete block boundaries occur, trailing NA cells placed. Use "row" operational movement follows rows. Use "column" operational movement follows columns. argument interacts directly serpentine. serpentine Logical indicating whether alternate rows columns reverse direction stream generation. TRUE, traversal alternates direction: row order = "row", column order = "column". Use field-book order mimic serpentine movement field. changes stream order, turn changes replicate incomplete block boundaries. Depends : order seed Optional integer seed reproducibility. NULL, seed generated internally returned seed_used. Use layout reproducible. affects: entry allocation, within-block arrangement, random check placement, optional dispersion optimization (unless separate dispersion seed supplied). attempts Integer giving number swap proposals used improving entry allocation across blocks within replicate. Larger values increase search effort change design algorithm . Use larger values : family structure strongly imbalanced, user wants better separation similar materials, number entries per replicate large. warn_and_correct Logical retained interface continuity. function, field dimensions fixed altered. argument included interface stays similar related design functions, drive field resizing . fix_rows Logical retained interface continuity. function, field dimensions fixed altered. Included mainly consistency related interfaces. cluster_source Character specifying grouping source used allocation arrangement. Allowed values: "Family" "GRM" "" Use "Family" user-supplied family labels adequate interpretable. Use "GRM" genomic structure define similarity precisely. Use "" pedigree relationships available define grouping. argument determines whether GRM, , id_map, cluster_method, cluster_seed, cluster_attempts, n_pcs_use become active. GRM Optional genomic relationship matrix. Required : cluster_source = "GRM", use_dispersion = TRUE dispersion_source = "GRM". Ignored otherwise. Use genomic relatedness influence grouping dispersion. Matrix row column names must match entry IDs, reachable id_map. Optional pedigree relationship matrix. Required : cluster_source = "", use_dispersion = TRUE dispersion_source = "". Ignored otherwise. Use pedigree relatedness influence grouping dispersion. id_map Optional data.frame columns Treatment LineID. Used cluster_source %% c("GRM", "") treatment IDs already match matrix row/column names. Use field-book treatment labels different matrix IDs. Example: treatment labels may breeder-friendly names GRM uses internal IDs. cluster_method Character specifying clustering method applied PCA matrix-based grouping. Allowed values: "kmeans" "hclust" Use "kmeans" reproducible compact clustering preferred. Use "hclust" hierarchical approach preferred. Active cluster_source %% c("GRM", ""). cluster_seed Integer seed k-means initialization. Active : cluster_source %% c("GRM", ""), cluster_method = "kmeans". Use make matrix-based grouping reproducible. cluster_attempts Integer number random starts k-means clustering. Active : cluster_source %% c("GRM", ""), cluster_method = "kmeans". Use larger values clustering stability important. n_pcs_use Integer Inf giving number principal components used matrix-based clustering. Ignored cluster_source = "Family". Use smaller values broad structure drive grouping. Use Inf function use many informative components possible. min_entry_slots_per_block Integer giving minimum number entry slots allowed incomplete block. argument constrains automatically chosen number blocks per replicate. Use smaller values blocks per replicate acceptable. Use larger values blocks become small checks inserted. particularly important checks numerous, repeated checks consume capacity every incomplete block. max_blocks_per_rep Optional integer giving upper bound number incomplete blocks per replicate. NULL, number blocks per replicate derived capacity constraints. Use user wants prevent design creating many incomplete blocks, even fit mathematically. Useful operational simplicity analysis preference favors fewer blocks. eval_efficiency Logical indicating whether efficiency diagnostics computed. Use TRUE user wants compare design quality mixed model. Use FALSE layout needed. FALSE, efficiency-related arguments ignored. treatment_effect Character indicating entries treated efficiency evaluation. Allowed values: "random" "fixed" Active eval_efficiency = TRUE. Use "fixed" interest lies contrast precision among entry means. Use "random" interest lies BLUP-style prediction quality. prediction_type Character controlling random-effect efficiency model. Allowed values: "none" "IID" "GBLUP" "PBLUP" Active : eval_efficiency = TRUE, treatment_effect = "random". Use "none" skip random-effect efficiency. Use "IID" entry random effects assumed independent. Use "GBLUP" "PBLUP" prediction use relationship matrix K. K Optional relationship matrix used two contexts: random-effect efficiency : eval_efficiency = TRUE, treatment_effect = "random", prediction_type %% c("GBLUP", "PBLUP"); dispersion optimization : use_dispersion = TRUE, dispersion_source = "K". Ignored otherwise. Use prediction dispersion based explicit relationship matrix. line_id_map Optional data.frame columns Treatment LineID. Used K active treatment labels differ rownames(K). serves role K id_map serves GRM . varcomp Named list variance components used efficiency evaluation. Must contain: sigma_e2 sigma_g2 sigma_rep2 sigma_ib2 sigma_r2 sigma_c2 Use realistic values efficiency reflect plausible trial model. Example: use larger sigma_ib2 incomplete blocks expected differ strongly. check_as_fixed Logical indicating whether checks included fixed indicators efficiency evaluation. Active eval_efficiency = TRUE. Use TRUE checks explicitly modeled benchmark fixed effects. residual_structure Character specifying residual correlation model. Allowed values: "IID" "AR1" "AR1xAR1" Active eval_efficiency = TRUE. Use "IID" spatial residual correlation assumed. Use "AR1" row-wise spatial correlation expected. Use "AR1xAR1" row column spatial correlation expected. rho_row Numeric AR1 row parameter. Active : eval_efficiency = TRUE, residual_structure %% c("AR1", "AR1xAR1"). Use values near 0 weak row correlation larger absolute values stronger correlation. rho_col Numeric AR1 column parameter. Active : eval_efficiency = TRUE, residual_structure = "AR1xAR1". Use column-wise correlation expected addition row-wise correlation. spatial_engine Character retained interface compatibility. Allowed values: "auto" "sparse" "dense" function efficiency code primarily uses sparse Matrix operations, argument retained interface remains aligned related functions. dense_max_n Integer retained interface compatibility. argument included consistency related functions. eff_trace_samples Integer number Hutchinson trace samples used approximate efficiency needed target treatment dimension exceeds eff_full_max. Larger values improve approximation stability increase runtime. eff_full_max Integer maximum target dimension exact efficiency extraction. threshold, function switches approximate trace-based method. Use larger values exact computation memory runtime allow. check_placement Character specifying check positions chosen within blocks. Allowed values: "systematic" "random" Use "systematic" checks approximately evenly spread block. Use "random" check positions may vary freely. check_position_pattern Argument retained interface compatibility. used current stream-based placement logic. use_dispersion Logical; TRUE, apply post-hoc dispersion optimization among non-check treatments. Use genetically pedigree-similar entries less clustered within replicates. FALSE, dispersion-related arguments ignored. dispersion_source Character selecting matrix used dispersion scoring. Allowed values: "K" "" "GRM" Active use_dispersion = TRUE. Use "K" dedicated relationship matrix preferred. Use "" pedigree-based dispersion. Use "GRM" genomic-based dispersion. dispersion_radius Integer neighborhood radius dispersion scoring. Two plots considered neighbors : max(|dr|, |dc|) <= dispersion_radius Use 1 immediate neighbors . Use larger values broader local dispersion matters. dispersion_iters Integer number swap proposals used dispersion search. Larger values increase optimization effort runtime. dispersion_seed Optional integer seed used dispersion search. Use dispersion optimization reproducible independently initial design seed. left default, function uses supplied value interface. verbose Logical; TRUE, print derived replicate block structure. Use diagnostics, debugging, understanding function chose replicate sizes incomplete block sizes.

title: Create a fixed-grid alpha row-column design with unequal incomplete blocks and checks in every block — alpha_rc_stream
text: list : layout_matrix character matrix size n_rows × n_cols. Unused cells NA. field_book plot-level data frame one row per field cell, including unused cells. efficiency NULL list efficiency summaries computed non-NA plots. seed_used actual random seed used internally. design_info list summarizing replicate sizes, incomplete block sizes, used unused cells, key settings.

title: Create a fixed-grid alpha row-column design with unequal incomplete blocks and checks in every block — alpha_rc_stream
text: Construct fixed-field row-column design full n_rows × n_cols grid first converted single ordered stream positions, split contiguous replicate segments, subdivided incomplete blocks possibly unequal size. Within replicate, every entry appears exactly , every incomplete block contains checks, unused field cells left trailing NA positions end global stream. Let: E = number entries, C = number checks, R = number replicates, b = number incomplete blocks per replicate. number used plots per replicate : E + b * C total number used plots : R * (E + b * C) total must fit within fixed field capacity: n_rows * n_cols function chooses block plan respects: number entries, number checks, minimum entry capacity per block, user-supplied cap blocks per replicate, total fixed field capacity. efficiency evaluation, function uses observed non-NA plots. Depending treatment_effect, returned metrics represent either: precision fixed treatment contrasts, average prediction error variance random treatment effects. arguments warn_and_correct, fix_rows, spatial_engine, dense_max_n, check_position_pattern retained interface continuity, even materially alter stream-based design logic.

title: Create a fixed-grid alpha row-column design with unequal incomplete blocks and checks in every block — alpha_rc_stream
text: key feature function stream-based rather rectangle-based. Instead cutting field rectangular replicate areas, function: lists grid cells single global order, cuts order n_reps contiguous replicate segments, cuts replicate segment incomplete blocks. useful : planting follows continuous field-book order, operational field movement row-wise column-wise, replicate boundaries administrative logistical rather geometric, user wants unused cells appear tail field stream. replicate: entry used exactly , incomplete block receives every check, remaining block capacity filled entries, allocation improved heuristically reduce local grouping conflicts.

what: What this function is most useful for
title: Create a fixed-grid alpha row-column design with unequal incomplete blocks and checks in every block — alpha_rc_stream
text: function particularly useful : field geometry fixed never auto-resized, design requires repeated checks every incomplete block, user wants row-column design without forcing rectangular replicate areas, practical planting harvesting follows stream order, family, pedigree, genomic structure influence entry placement, user wants evaluate resulting design using mixed-model framework.

title: Create a fixed-grid alpha row-column design with unequal incomplete blocks and checks in every block — alpha_rc_stream
text: Many arguments active particular modes. Grouping source cluster_source = "Family": grouping comes directly check_families entry_families, GRM, , id_map, cluster_method, cluster_seed, cluster_attempts, n_pcs_use ignored. cluster_source = "GRM": GRM required, id_map needed entry names differ rownames(GRM), grouping labels derived PCA followed clustering. cluster_source = "": required, id_map needed entry names differ rownames(), grouping labels derived PCA followed clustering. Derived block structure min_entry_slots_per_block controls minimum number non-check entry slots permitted incomplete block. max_blocks_per_rep optionally limits number blocks per replicate. max_blocks_per_rep = NULL, block count derived capacity min_entry_slots_per_block. Efficiency evaluation eval_efficiency = FALSE, efficiency arguments ignored. eval_efficiency = TRUE treatment_effect = "fixed": fixed-treatment contrast precision computed, prediction_type, K, line_id_map ignored. eval_efficiency = TRUE treatment_effect = "random": prediction_type becomes active. prediction_type = "none": random-effect efficiency skipped. prediction_type = "IID": entry random effects treated independent, K line_id_map ignored. prediction_type %% c("GBLUP", "PBLUP"): K required, line_id_map may required treatment IDs differ rownames(K). Residual structure residual_structure = "IID": rho_row rho_col ignored. residual_structure = "AR1": rho_row used. residual_structure = "AR1xAR1": rho_row rho_col used. Dispersion optimization use_dispersion = FALSE, dispersion arguments ignored. use_dispersion = TRUE, dispersion_source selects matrix used. dispersion_source = "K": K required, line_id_map may needed treatment IDs differ matrix row names. dispersion_source = "": required. dispersion_source = "GRM": GRM required. Check placement check_placement = "systematic" spreads checks approximately evenly within block stream. check_placement = "random" randomizes check positions. check_position_pattern retained interface continuity used current stream-based placement logic.

title: Create a fixed-grid alpha row-column design with unequal incomplete blocks and checks in every block — alpha_rc_stream
code: data("OptiDesign_example_data", package = "OptiDesign") x <- OptiDesign_example_data  ## Family-based row-column stream design out_alpha_family <- do.call(   alpha_rc_stream,   c(     x$OptiDesign_alpha_example,     x$OptiDesign_alpha_args_family   ) )  dim(out_alpha_family$layout_matrix) #> [1] 12 14 head(out_alpha_family$field_book) #>   Treatment Family Gcluster Check PlotStream Rep IBlock BlockInRep Row Column #> 1      L140    F24     <NA> FALSE          1   1      1          1   1      1 #> 2      L103    F14     <NA>  TRUE          2   1      1          1   1      2 #> 3      L102    F17     <NA>  TRUE          3   1      1          1   1      3 #> 4      L117    F06     <NA> FALSE          4   1      1          1   1      4 #> 5      L101    F14     <NA>  TRUE          5   1      1          1   1      5 #> 6      L133    F22     <NA> FALSE          6   1      1          1   1      6 out_alpha_family$design_info$n_blocks_per_rep #> [1] 6  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## GRM-based row-column stream design with dispersion out_alpha_grm <- do.call(   alpha_rc_stream,   c(     x$OptiDesign_alpha_example,     x$OptiDesign_alpha_args_grm   ) )  dim(out_alpha_grm$layout_matrix) head(out_alpha_grm$field_book) out_alpha_grm$efficiency } # }

what: OptiDesign: Experimental Field Design Utilities for Optimized Layout Construction — OptiDesign-package
title: OptiDesign: Experimental Field Design Utilities for Optimized Layout Construction — OptiDesign-package
text: OptiDesign provides tools constructing optimized experimental field designs plant breeding related applications. package focuses layouts field structure, treatment grouping, optional mixed-model evaluation integrated design stage rather treated separate downstream tasks.

title: OptiDesign: Experimental Field Design Utilities for Optimized Layout Construction — OptiDesign-package
text: package currently includes two main design constructors: prep_famoptg() repeated-check block designs flexible replication, supporting augmented designs, partially replicated (p-rep) designs, RCBD-type repeated-check designs. function allows optional grouping family labels relationship matrices, optional dispersion optimization, optional mixed-model efficiency evaluation. alpha_rc_stream() fixed-grid alpha row-column stream designs field treated global traversal stream, replicates contiguous stream segments, incomplete blocks may unequal, checks included every block, unused cells appear end stream. package intended users need designs feasible field, also informed : family structure, pedigree genomic relationship matrices, spatial distribution related materials, mixed-model design efficiency considerations.

title: OptiDesign: Experimental Field Design Utilities for Optimized Layout Construction — OptiDesign-package
text: OptiDesign particularly useful user wants : construct designs repeated checks heterogeneous entry replication, generate augmented, p-rep, balanced repeated-check layouts, avoid local clustering related entries, use family, pedigree, genomic structure guide placement, preserve realistic field-book order layout constraints, evaluate design fixed- random-effect mixed models, compare alternative layout strategies field implementation.

title: OptiDesign: Experimental Field Design Utilities for Optimized Layout Construction — OptiDesign-package
text: Several design routines package can use one three grouping sources: direct family labels, genomic relationship matrix (GRM), pedigree relationship matrix (). addition, workflows may use separate relationship matrix K : BLUP-style prediction efficiency calculations, dispersion optimization. treatment names match matrix row names directly, mapping tables id_map line_id_map can used reconcile identifiers.

title: Example data for OptiDesign — OptiDesign_example_data
text: named list following components: OptiDesign_lines data frame columns Treatment Family representing synthetic pool line identifiers family labels. OptiDesign_id_map data frame columns Treatment LineID used map treatment labels row column names relationship matrices names differ. OptiDesign_GRM synthetic genomic relationship matrix rownames colnames corresponding line IDs. can used matrix-based grouping genomic dispersion examples. OptiDesign_A synthetic pedigree-style relationship matrix rownames colnames corresponding line IDs. can used pedigree-based grouping dispersion examples. OptiDesign_K synthetic relationship kernel matrix rownames colnames corresponding line IDs. intended workflows require K matrix, BLUP-style prediction settings dispersion optimization using dispersion_source = "K". OptiDesign_famoptg_example named list containing core treatment vectors field dimensions needed run prep_famoptg(). includes checks, p-rep treatments, unreplicated treatments, basic field dimensions. OptiDesign_alpha_example named list containing core treatment vectors field dimensions needed run alpha_rc_stream(). includes checks, entries, number replicates, fixed field dimensions. OptiDesign_famoptg_args_family named list additional arguments family-based call prep_famoptg(). illustrates simple design setup without efficiency evaluation. OptiDesign_famoptg_args_grm named list additional arguments GRM-based call prep_famoptg(). illustrates matrix-based grouping optional dispersion settings. OptiDesign_alpha_args_family named list additional arguments family-based call alpha_rc_stream(). illustrates stream-based row-column design without efficiency evaluation. OptiDesign_alpha_args_grm named list additional arguments GRM-based call alpha_rc_stream(). illustrates matrix-based grouping optional dispersion settings.

Load dataset : data("OptiDesign_example_data", package = "OptiDesign") extract individual components needed, example: x <- OptiDesign_example_data x$OptiDesign_famoptg_example x$OptiDesign_GRM x$OptiDesign_alpha_args_family

what: Create a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — prep_famoptg
title: Create a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — prep_famoptg
text: prep_famoptg() general repeated-check block design constructor check treatments included every block, non-check treatments allocated across blocks according user-specified replication levels. Depending supplied treatment structure, function can generate: augmented design, checks repeated entries unreplicated; partially replicated (p-rep) design, non-check entries replicated others unreplicated; RCBD-type repeated-check design, non-check entries given replication number greater 1, especially replication equals n_blocks. basic allocation rules : Checks included every block. Replicated non-check treatments assigned subset blocks may appear overall, within single block. Unreplicated treatments appear exactly full design. means, example, treatment replication 2 n_blocks >= 2, two replicates placed two different blocks rather repeated within block. function returns: layout matrix representing field grid, field book giving one row per assigned plot treatment identity, grouping metadata, block membership, field coordinates. Optionally, function can also: derive treatment groups genomic relationship matrix (GRM) pedigree relationship matrix (); optimize spatial dispersion checks; apply swap-based local search reduce local relatedness among nearby non-check plots; compute mixed-model design efficiency metrics fixed random treatment effects.

title: Create a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — prep_famoptg
code: prep_famoptg(   check_treatments,   check_families,   p_rep_treatments,   p_rep_reps,   p_rep_families,   unreplicated_treatments,   unreplicated_families,   n_blocks,   n_rows,   n_cols,   order = "column",   serpentine = FALSE,   seed = NULL,   attempts = 1000,   warn_and_correct = TRUE,   fix_rows = TRUE,   cluster_source = c("Family", "GRM", "A"),   GRM = NULL,   A = NULL,   id_map = NULL,   cluster_method = c("kmeans", "hclust"),   cluster_seed = 1,   cluster_attempts = 25,   n_pcs_use = Inf,   eval_efficiency = FALSE,   treatment_effect = c("random", "fixed"),   prediction_type = c("none", "IID", "GBLUP", "PBLUP"),   K = NULL,   line_id_map = NULL,   varcomp = list(sigma_e2 = 1, sigma_g2 = 1, sigma_b2 = 1, sigma_r2 = 1, sigma_c2 = 1),   check_as_fixed = TRUE,   residual_structure = c("IID", "AR1", "AR1xAR1"),   rho_row = 0,   rho_col = 0,   spatial_engine = c("auto", "sparse", "dense"),   dense_max_n = 5000,   eff_trace_samples = 80,   eff_full_max = 400,   check_placement = c("random", "systematic", "optimal"),   check_opt_attempts = 200,   use_dispersion = FALSE,   dispersion_source = c("K", "A", "GRM"),   dispersion_radius = 1,   dispersion_iters = 2000,   dispersion_seed = 1 )

title: Create a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — prep_famoptg
text: check_treatments Character vector check treatment identifiers. treatments included every block, define repeated reference structure design. Use argument standard checks, control varieties, benchmark cultivars, fixed reference entries must present everywhere improve block comparability. argument always required. Example use case: breeding trial 3 control cultivars included blocks. check_families Character vector length check_treatments. Provides family group label check treatment. Even cluster_source != "Family", checks still need family labels checks always assigned group label output remain part grouping metadata used internally. argument always depends check_treatments must align exactly order length. Example: three checks belong control class, check_families might c("CHECK", "CHECK", "CHECK"). p_rep_treatments Character vector replicated non-check treatment IDs. entries appear overall, given block. Use argument non-check treatments replicated. Depending used: empty unreplicated_treatments supplied, function behaves augmented repeated-check constructor; contains non-check entries, function behaves p-rep repeated-check constructor; contains non-check entries common replication greater 1, function behaves RCBD-type repeated-check constructor. Can NULL character(0) replicated non-check treatments present. Depends : p_rep_reps p_rep_families Example use case: elite lines replicated twice many lines remain unreplicated. p_rep_reps Integer vector giving total number replicates entry p_rep_treatments. controls many times replicated non-check treatment appears full design. Must: length p_rep_treatments; align element-wise p_rep_treatments; satisfy p_rep_reps[] <= n_blocks, treatment occur twice block. treatment replication 2 n_blocks >= 2 placed two different blocks. Use common vector rep(2, length(p_rep_treatments)) replicated treatments appear twice. Use unequal values entries deserve replication others. p_rep_families Character vector length p_rep_treatments. Family group labels replicated non-check treatments. labels required whenever replicated non-check treatments exist. Even cluster_source != "Family", remain useful function uses non-check family structure determine target number clusters matrix-based grouping modes. Depends : p_rep_treatments Example use case: replicated candidate lines belong multiple families user wants adjacency control reflect structure. unreplicated_treatments Character vector treatments appear exactly . single-plot entries used broad screening needed full replication possible. Use NULL character(0) unreplicated treatments present. combination repeated checks replicated non-checks, entries define augmented repeated-check design. Depends : unreplicated_families Example use case: large early-generation nursery new line observed . unreplicated_families Character vector length unreplicated_treatments. Family group labels unreplicated treatments. Required whenever unreplicated_treatments provided. labels especially useful : cluster_source = "Family"; matrix-based grouping used target number clusters reflect family structure among non-checks. n_blocks Integer giving number experimental blocks. determines many times checks repeated, also constrains maximum allowable replication count replicated non-check treatment. Use blocks : many checks must accommodated; finer local control desired; field heterogeneity suggests stronger blocking. argument affects: total plot count; feasibility replicated-treatment assignment; distribution unreplicated treatments. replicated treatment appear every block, replication must equal n_blocks. n_rows Integer giving number rows final field grid. Controls physical field geometry returned layout_matrix. Use match intended number field rows. warn_and_correct = TRUE, value may retained n_cols adjusted fix_rows = TRUE. n_cols Integer giving number columns final field grid. Controls physical field geometry returned layout_matrix. Use together n_rows represent intended field dimensions. warn_and_correct = TRUE, value may retained n_rows adjusted fix_rows = FALSE. order Character specifying field grid filled. Allowed values: "row": fill row row; "column": fill column column. Use "row" field-book order planting order follows rows. Use "column" field-book order follows columns. argument interacts directly serpentine. serpentine Logical indicating whether alternate rows columns reverse direction grid filling. TRUE: order = "row", alternate rows reversed; order = "column", alternate columns reversed. Use planting, labeling, harvesting follows serpentine movement pattern. affects final ordered treatments mapped space; change block composition. Depends : order seed Optional integer seed controlling stochastic components design. Use reproducibility important. NULL, seed generated internally returned seed_used. seed affects: replicated-treatment block assignment; shuffling within blocks; candidate generation optimal check placement; optional dispersion optimization dispersion_seed NULL. attempts Integer giving maximum number shuffle attempts per block. Used reduce adjacency -group entries 1D block ordering. Larger values may improve adjacency avoidance increase runtime. Use larger values : family structure highly imbalanced; many similar entries occur block; stricter adjacency avoidance desired. warn_and_correct Logical controlling happens supplied field size match required number plots. FALSE, function stops error. TRUE, function adjusts one dimension upward field can contain required plots. Use FALSE field dimensions fixed must changed. Use TRUE near-feasible layout repaired automatically. Depends : fix_rows fix_rows Logical used warn_and_correct = TRUE. TRUE, n_rows kept fixed n_cols adjusted. FALSE, n_cols kept fixed n_rows adjusted. Use according field dimension physically fixed practice. Example: field fixed number rows flexible row length, use fix_rows = TRUE. cluster_source Character specifying source grouping used non-check adjacency control. Allowed values: "Family" "GRM" "" Use "Family" family labels trusted easy interpret. Use "GRM" genomic relatedness define grouping accurately. Use "" pedigree structure available pedigree-based grouping desired. argument determines following become active: GRM id_map cluster_method cluster_seed cluster_attempts n_pcs_use GRM Optional square genomic relationship matrix rownames colnames. Required : cluster_source = "GRM", use_dispersion = TRUE dispersion_source = "GRM". Ignored otherwise. Use genomic similarity drive grouping dispersion scoring. Row column names must correspond treatment IDs IDs reachable via id_map. Optional square pedigree relationship matrix rownames colnames. Required : cluster_source = "", use_dispersion = TRUE dispersion_source = "". Ignored otherwise. Use pedigree structure drive grouping dispersion scoring. id_map Optional data frame columns Treatment LineID. Used cluster_source %% c("GRM", "") treatment labels design exactly match rownames selected relationship matrix. Use : treatment names design breeder-friendly labels; matrix uses internal IDs, numeric codes, standardized line names. Ignored cluster_source = "Family". cluster_method Clustering method used PCA matrix-based grouping. Allowed values: "kmeans" "hclust" Use "kmeans" compact clusters acceptable reproducible initialization desired. Use "hclust" hierarchical grouping preferred. Active cluster_source %% c("GRM", ""). cluster_seed Integer seed used cluster_method = "kmeans". Ignored otherwise. Use k-means clustering must reproducible. cluster_attempts Integer number random starts passed kmeans(). Active : cluster_source %% c("GRM", ""), cluster_method = "kmeans". Larger values may improve clustering stability higher runtime cost. n_pcs_use Integer Inf giving number principal components used PCA-based clustering matrix-based grouping. Use smaller value : leading structure define grouping; smaller PCs considered mostly noise. Use Inf let function use many informative PCs available. Ignored cluster_source = "Family". eval_efficiency Logical; TRUE, compute design efficiency metrics. Use TRUE design evaluated mixed-model framework. Use FALSE layout needed. argument activates: treatment_effect prediction_type K line_id_map varcomp check_as_fixed residual_structure rho_row rho_col spatial_engine dense_max_n eff_trace_samples eff_full_max treatment_effect Character indicating whether non-check treatments treated "fixed" "random" efficiency evaluation. Active eval_efficiency = TRUE. Use "fixed" interest precision estimated treatment contrasts. Use "random" interest prediction quality treatment effects. prediction_type Character controlling random-effect prediction model. Allowed values: "none" "IID" "GBLUP" "PBLUP" Active : eval_efficiency = TRUE, treatment_effect = "random". Use "none" skip random-effect efficiency. Use "IID" non-check effects assumed independent. Use "GBLUP" "PBLUP" prediction use relationship matrix K. K Optional square relationship matrix. Used two contexts: efficiency evaluation random effects predicted using "GBLUP" "PBLUP"; dispersion optimization dispersion_source = "K". Ignored otherwise. Use prediction dispersion based explicit relationship matrix. line_id_map Optional data frame columns Treatment LineID. Used K active treatment names differ rownames(K). analogous id_map, specifically matrix K. Use design uses one set treatment labels K uses another. varcomp Named list variance components used efficiency evaluation. Must contain: sigma_e2 sigma_g2 sigma_b2 sigma_r2 sigma_c2 Active eval_efficiency = TRUE. Use realistic values efficiency metric reflect plausible data-generating model. Example use case: user may set larger sigma_e2 expecting noisy phenotypes, larger sigma_b2 block--block heterogeneity substantial. check_as_fixed Logical indicating whether checks included fixed indicator columns efficiency evaluation. Active eval_efficiency = TRUE. Use TRUE checks explicitly modeled fixed benchmark effects. residual_structure Character specifying residual correlation model. Allowed values: "IID" "AR1" "AR1xAR1" Use "IID" spatial residual correlation assumed. Use "AR1" correlation expected mainly along rows. Use "AR1xAR1" correlation expected along rows columns. Active eval_efficiency = TRUE. rho_row Numeric AR1 row correlation parameter. Active : eval_efficiency = TRUE, residual_structure %% c("AR1", "AR1xAR1"). Use values near 0 weak row correlation values closer 1 magnitude stronger row correlation. rho_col Numeric AR1 column correlation parameter. Active : eval_efficiency = TRUE, residual_structure = "AR1xAR1". Use column-wise spatial correlation part assumed residual model. spatial_engine Character controlling computational implementation spatial residual calculations. Allowed values: "auto" "dense" "sparse" Active : eval_efficiency = TRUE, residual_structure != "IID". Use "auto" cases. Use "dense" smaller problems dense linear algebra acceptable. Use "sparse" larger problems sparse precision matrices preferred. dense_max_n Integer threshold used spatial_engine = "auto". number observed plots threshold, dense computation used; otherwise sparse computation used. eff_trace_samples Integer giving number Hutchinson trace samples used approximate efficiency evaluation needed. Active : eval_efficiency = TRUE, target dimension exceeds eff_full_max. Larger values improve stability approximation increase runtime. eff_full_max Integer giving maximum target dimension exact inverse sub-block extraction efficiency evaluation. Active eval_efficiency = TRUE. Use larger values exact evaluation desired computation affordable. Use smaller values switch approximation earlier reduce runtime. check_placement Character specifying checks placed within blocks. Allowed values: "random" "systematic" "optimal" Use "random" explicit spacing pattern required. Use "systematic" checks spread approximately evenly within block. Use "optimal" spatial dispersion checks final 2D grid priority. "optimal" selected, check_opt_attempts becomes active. check_opt_attempts Integer number candidate layouts evaluated check_placement = "optimal". Larger values may improve final spatial spread checks increase runtime. Ignored unless check_placement = "optimal". use_dispersion Logical; TRUE, apply post-layout swap optimization reduce local relatedness among nearby non-check treatments. Use genetically pedigree-similar entries less clustered final field. FALSE, dispersion-related arguments ignored. dispersion_source Character specifying matrix used dispersion optimization. Allowed values: "K" "" "GRM" Active use_dispersion = TRUE. Use "K" dedicated relationship matrix dispersion available. Use "" pedigree-based spacing. Use "GRM" genomic-based spacing. dispersion_radius Integer neighborhood radius used dispersion scoring. Neighboring plots defined using Chebyshev distance, meaning plots neighbors max(|dr|, |dc|) <= dispersion_radius. Use 1 immediate neighbors . Use larger values broader local neighborhoods matter. Active use_dispersion = TRUE. dispersion_iters Integer number swap proposals used local search. Larger values allow extensive search reduced local relatedness increase runtime. Active use_dispersion = TRUE. dispersion_seed Optional integer seed used dispersion optimization. Active use_dispersion = TRUE. NULL, function uses seed_used. Use user wants reproducibility dispersion step independently initial design seed.

title: Create a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — prep_famoptg
text: Construct augmented, partially replicated, RCBD-type repeated-check field design optional treatment grouping, optional post-layout dispersion optimization, optional mixed-model efficiency diagnostics. function uses mod() imported pracma package serpentine traversal logic. Field dimensions match exact number required plots warn_and_correct = TRUE. case, function expands one dimension field can contain required plots. Extra cells remain NA returned layout_matrix, field_book includes assigned plots. function separates: construction logic (treatments placed); grouping logic (similarity defined); dispersion logic (nearby related entries discouraged); efficiency logic (final design evaluated). separation important user may, example: use family labels adjacency control; use genomic matrix dispersion optimization; choose compute efficiency; alternatively: use GRM-based clustering; skip dispersion optimization; compute GBLUP-based efficiency using separate matrix K.

what: Design classes represented by the same function
title: Create a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — prep_famoptg
text: 1. Augmented repeated-check design obtained : p_rep_treatments = NULL character(0), p_rep_reps = NULL integer(0), p_rep_families = NULL character(0), non-check entries supplied unreplicated_treatments. case: checks repeated every block; test entries appear ; design behaves augmented repeated-check layout. setting especially useful early-stage screening many entries must observed checks can repeated systematically. 2. Partially replicated repeated-check design obtained : non-check treatments supplied p_rep_treatments; treatments replication counts greater 1; entries may remain unreplicated. classical use case function: mixture repeated checks, replicated candidate entries, single-plot candidate entries. 3. RCBD-type repeated-check design obtained : non-check treatments supplied p_rep_treatments; receive replication number greater 1. common replication equals n_blocks, every non-check treatment appears every block, closest repeated-check analogue classical RCBD framework. common replication less n_blocks, design remains balanced repeated, strict classical RCBD every treatment appears every block.

title: Create a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — prep_famoptg
text: function proceeds several stages: Validate inputs reconcile field size required number plots. Prepare grouping information family labels clustering GRM / . Assign replicated non-check entries blocks subject rule treatment appears twice block. Distribute unreplicated entries across blocks. Insert checks block using chosen placement strategy. Shuffle treatment order within blocks reduce adjacency -group entries 1D block ordering. Map ordered treatments field grid according order serpentine. Optionally apply genetic dispersion optimization. Optionally compute design efficiency metrics.

what: What this function is most useful for

text: function especially useful : early- intermediate-stage breeding trials many entries must screened can equally replicated; augmented repeated-check designs large number single-plot test entries; p-rep layouts mixing replicated unreplicated entries; balanced repeated-check block designs candidate entries replicated; experiments checks must appear every block; layouts related lines clustered closely; applications family, pedigree, genomic relationships influence physical layout construction; cases user wants compare alternative design choices using model-based efficiency metrics.

what: Grouping logic and why it matters
title: Create a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — prep_famoptg
text: Several parts function rely idea group: block construction, groups used reduce adjacency similar entries 1D ordering within block. optional dispersion optimization, relationship matrices used discourage close spatial placement related non-check entries. Group labels may come : user-supplied families (cluster_source = "Family"); clusters derived genomic relationship matrix (cluster_source = "GRM"); clusters derived pedigree relationship matrix (cluster_source = ""). Family-based grouping simplest option usually appropriate family labels meaningful easy interpret. Matrix-based grouping useful : family labels coarse; genomic pedigree structure informative nominal family labels; objective spread genetically similar materials effectively.

title: Create a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — prep_famoptg
text: Many arguments active certain modes. Treatment structure p_rep_treatments empty (NULL) unreplicated_treatments non-empty, function behaves augmented repeated-check design constructor. p_rep_treatments unreplicated_treatments present, function behaves p-rep repeated-check design constructor. non-check treatments supplied p_rep_treatments common replication number greater 1, function behaves RCBD-type repeated-check design constructor. Grouping mode cluster_source = "Family": check_families, p_rep_families, unreplicated_families used. GRM, , id_map, cluster_method, cluster_seed, cluster_attempts, n_pcs_use ignored. cluster_source = "GRM": GRM required. id_map needed treatment IDs match rownames(GRM). cluster_method, cluster_seed, cluster_attempts, n_pcs_use used. ignored. cluster_source = "": required. id_map needed treatment IDs match rownames(). cluster_method, cluster_seed, cluster_attempts, n_pcs_use used. GRM ignored. Efficiency evaluation eval_efficiency = FALSE, efficiency-related arguments ignored. eval_efficiency = TRUE treatment_effect = "fixed": fixed-effect design efficiency computed; prediction_type, K, line_id_map ignored. eval_efficiency = TRUE treatment_effect = "random": prediction_type becomes active. prediction_type = "none": random-effect prediction efficiency computed. prediction_type = "IID": non-check treatments treated independent random effects; K line_id_map ignored. prediction_type %% c("GBLUP", "PBLUP"): K required; line_id_map may needed treatment IDs match rownames(K). Residual structure residual_structure = "IID": residuals assumed independent; rho_row rho_col ignored. residual_structure = "AR1": row-wise correlation used; rho_row active; rho_col ignored. residual_structure = "AR1xAR1": row column correlations used; rho_row rho_col active. Check placement check_placement = "optimal", check_opt_attempts used. Otherwise, check_opt_attempts ignored. Dispersion optimization use_dispersion = FALSE, dispersion arguments ignored. use_dispersion = TRUE dispersion_source = "K": K required; line_id_map may needed treatment IDs differ rownames(K). use_dispersion = TRUE dispersion_source = "": required. use_dispersion = TRUE dispersion_source = "GRM": GRM required.

title: Create a repeated-check block design with flexible replication — prep_famoptg
code: data("OptiDesign_example_data", package = "OptiDesign") x <- OptiDesign_example_data  ## --------------------------------------------------------- ## Example 1: Family-based repeated-check design ## This example uses the shipped family-based arguments. ## Depending on the supplied treatment lists, the same function ## can represent augmented, p-rep, or RCBD-type repeated-check ## layouts. ## --------------------------------------------------------- out_family <- do.call(   prep_famoptg,   c(     x$OptiDesign_famoptg_example,     x$OptiDesign_famoptg_args_family   ) )  dim(out_family$layout_matrix) #> [1] 16  8 head(out_family$field_book) #>        Treatment Family Gcluster Block Plot Row Column #> plot_1      L076    F03     <NA>     1    1   1      1 #> plot_2      L061    F16     <NA>     1    2   2      1 #> plot_3      L028    F12     <NA>     1    3   3      1 #> plot_4      L030    F10     <NA>     1    4   4      1 #> plot_5      L027    F21     <NA>     1    5   5      1 #> plot_6      L051    F09     <NA>     1    6   6      1 out_family$seed_used #> [1] 123  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## --------------------------------------------------------- ## Example 2: GRM-based grouping with dispersion optimization ## --------------------------------------------------------- out_grm <- do.call(   prep_famoptg,   c(     x$OptiDesign_famoptg_example,     x$OptiDesign_famoptg_args_grm   ) )  dim(out_grm$layout_matrix) head(out_grm$field_book) out_grm$efficiency  ## --------------------------------------------------------- ## Example 3: Augmented repeated-check use pattern ## In practice, this is obtained by leaving p-rep arguments empty ## and supplying candidate entries only as unreplicated entries. ## --------------------------------------------------------- aug_args <- x$OptiDesign_famoptg_example aug_args$p_rep_treatments <- character(0) aug_args$p_rep_reps <- integer(0) aug_args$p_rep_families <- character(0)  out_aug <- do.call(   prep_famoptg,   c(aug_args, x$OptiDesign_famoptg_args_family) )  dim(out_aug$layout_matrix) head(out_aug$field_book)  ## --------------------------------------------------------- ## Example 4: RCBD-type repeated-check use pattern ## Here all non-check entries are treated as replicated entries. ## If their common replication equals n_blocks, each entry appears ## once in every block. ## --------------------------------------------------------- rcbd_args <- x$OptiDesign_famoptg_example all_noncheck <- c(rcbd_args$p_rep_treatments, rcbd_args$unreplicated_treatments) all_noncheck_fam <- c(rcbd_args$p_rep_families, rcbd_args$unreplicated_families)  rcbd_args$p_rep_treatments <- all_noncheck rcbd_args$p_rep_reps <- rep(2L, length(all_noncheck)) rcbd_args$p_rep_families <- all_noncheck_fam rcbd_args$unreplicated_treatments <- character(0) rcbd_args$unreplicated_families <- character(0)  out_rcbd_type <- do.call(   prep_famoptg,   c(rcbd_args, x$OptiDesign_famoptg_args_family) )  dim(out_rcbd_type$layout_matrix) head(out_rcbd_type$field_book) } # }

title: OptiDesign 0.1.0
text: Initial package structure. Added prep_famoptg(). Added alpha_rc_stream(). Added package documentation, tests, pkgdown configuration, GitHub Actions workflow.
